title: Fix Dashboard due/credit calculations, enforce unique client reference, and add totals row in Client Database

requirements.text: Fix the Dashboard card "Due (completed appointments; current month)" so that its computed amount exactly matches the Monthly Listing table’s total "Negative Credit" for the same month (e.g., February must show 0 when February’s Monthly Listing "Negative Credit" total is 0).

quotes: Page « Tableau de bord », Cadre « Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant) » le calcul ... au mois de février n’est pas correct ... dans ... « Listing Mensuel » du mois de février ... zéro ... mais ... « Dus ... » il y a 4400

acceptanceCriteria: When the Monthly Listing for February displays a total Negative Credit of 0, the Dashboard "Due (completed appointments; current month)" card displays 0 for February as well.
Dashboard due computation uses the same month selection and the same underlying credit logic as the Monthly Listing totals (no divergence between components).
No regressions: the Dashboard due card remains consistent with the Monthly Listing totals for all other months when navigating/refreshing.

requirements.text: Fix the Monthly Listing "Previous month credit" carryover so that for every month of the year (including from March onward), the "Previous month credit" column equals the ending credit/balance from the immediately preceding month, matching the preceding month’s displayed credit amounts.

quotes: Page « Tableau de bord », cadre « Listing Mensuel » ... jusqu’au mois de février ... reprend ... du mois précédent ... Mais à partir du mois de mars il ne reporte pas les mêmes montants. Il faut corriger ...

acceptanceCriteria: For any displayed month M, each client row’s "Previous month credit" equals the same client’s ending credit/balance from month M-1.
March’s "Previous month credit" values match February’s ending credit/balance values for the same clients.
This carryover remains correct for all months (April uses March, etc.), including January using December of the previous year when available.

requirements.text: Enforce uniqueness of the client reference field during both client creation (Client Database) and appointment creation. If a reference is already in use, prevent saving and show a clear validation error indicating the reference is already used.

quotes: Le champ référence, dans la création de nouveau rendez-vous et création base client, ne doit pas avoir 2 fois la même référence sinon, il faut indiquer lors de la création que cette référence est déjà utilisé.

acceptanceCriteria: Attempting to create a client with a referenceClient that already exists for the same signed-in user is rejected (backend validation) and the UI shows an error.

Attempting to create an appointment using a client reference that conflicts with an existing client reference rule is rejected according to the same uniqueness policy (backend validation) and the UI shows an error.
Uniqueness is enforced server-side (cannot be bypassed via direct calls).
The existing behavior that prevents changing a client reference after creation remains unchanged.

requirements.text: On the Client Database page, add a totals row directly under the table headers that shows the grand total (sum) of the "Paid this year" column across all displayed clients.

quotes: Page « Base Client » Tableau « Liste des clients » il faut, en haut du tableau sous les noms des colonnes, ajouter une ligne totale qui indique la somme total de la colonne « Payé cette année »

acceptanceCriteria: A totals row is visible immediately under the column headers in the client list table.
The totals row displays the correct sum of "Paid this year" across all clients currently shown in the table (respecting any active sorting/filtering that affects which rows are displayed).
The total updates correctly when clients/appointments data changes (e.g., after adding/editing appointments or clients).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional backend services).
Do not modify files listed as immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (compose UI using existing components instead).
nonGoals: Adding new authentication methods (Internet Identity remains the only auth).
Adding external databases or third-party services.
Redesigning unrelated UI pages or changing existing accounting formulas beyond what is needed to restore consistency and correct carryover.
